survivors_flagged.one_line: Definitive merger agreement Feb 2, 2026 — Columbia Financial (CLBK) acquiring Northfield for ~$597M; live merger-arb pending shareholder/regulatory close.
Neurogene NGN-401 Rett: BTD granted Feb 2026, Embolden registrational dosing completes Q2 2026 + interim Phase 1/2 data mid-2026 → BLA; dated binary with asymmetry.

Active strategic-alternatives review: APT segment + Road Markings up for sale ('New Ingevity' simplification), Industrial Specialties/Ozark already closed; real ongoing portfolio monetization but no single dated hard milestone.
Matawinie graphite: $335M senior debt secured + Canada/Eni offtakes, Becancour FID expected H2 2026 — dated milestone but financing/FID risk, not classic special-sit asymmetry.

Live, contested FCC 900 MHz rulemaking (NPRM was at OMB, expected ~Apr-30 meeting; June 4 House hearing) — a genuine dated regulatory binary on the spectrum swap with mispriced asymmetry, decision still pending.

Murchinson-driven cash-rich shell running Guggenheim/Houlihan strategic review; Markforged sold to Stratasys, 'clear path' guided Q2 2026 — real activist+monetization process, dated but messy/diluted.
Definitive $750m de-SPAC with Abra Financial (Mar 2026, ABRX); shareholder vote/close pending but heavy-redemption/crypto-RIA risk caps it.
Bexobrutideg registrational path: Ph2 DAYBreak accelerated-approval data + Ph3 CLL-306 start midyear 2026; real dated binary, but a clinical (not classic special-sit) bet.
Public Storage all-stock buyout (0.14 PSA, ~$41.68); boards approved, holder vote + close expected Q3 2026 — clean spread arb.
Union Pacific transcontinental merger live at STB (amended app accepted May 2026, close early 2027); regulatory-binary spread.
Definitive $1.8B CIBC Caribbean acquisition signed May 2026, closes H1 2027 pending regulatory/shareholder votes — but NTB is the acquirer, not an arb target; structural re-rating, not a takeout.
Hard Sept 18, 2026 PDUFA for zidesamtinib (ROS1 NSCLC) plus neladalkib ALK NDA filing H2 2026 — two dated forward binaries with a commercial launch on deck.
One real dated binary: pivotal Phase 3 OPERA-01 palazestrant monotherapy topline expected FALL 2026 in ER+/HER2- mBC — forward, unfired, asymmetric for a sub-$1B oncology name.

verified.catalyst: Columbia Financial (CLBK) acquisition of Northfield Bancorp (~$597M, announced Feb 2 2026); deal-close pending early Q3 2026 after the merger cleared its shareholder vote and Fed/OCC approvals.
verified.dated_milestone: Deal close / second-step conversion expected early Q3 2026 (July 2026); shareholder vote already approved June 25, 2026; Fed/OCC approvals received May 4-8, 2026.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Columbia Financial/CLBK) + G2 (signed definitive merger agreement, concrete commitment)
verified.edge: L — both the major de-risking gates have already fired (shareholder approval 6/25/26 + Fed/OCC approvals 5/4-5/8/26), and NFBK at $14.64 trades at/above the $14.25–$14.65 cash-election floor, so the merger-arb spread is essentially closed/negative; no remaining unpriced figure.
verified.primary_source: NFBK DEFM14A merger proxy + Columbia GlobeNewswire regulatory-approval release (May 11, 2026) + NFBK 8-K shareholder-approval results (June 25, 2026); FMP quote NFBK $14.64 / CLBK $21.03 (June 29, 2026).
verified.analysis: The merger is real, named, and dated (CLBK acquiring NFBK, ~$597M, definitive agreement Feb 2 2026, close expected early Q3 2026), satisfying Bloom G1+G2. However, the catalyst is effectively fired/re-rated: NFBK shareholders approved on June 25, 2026 and the Fed/OCC granted regulatory approvals in early May 2026, so the binary risk gates are already cleared. With NFBK at $14.64 trading at or above the $14.25–$14.65 cash-consideration floor (1.425–1.465 CLBK exchange option, 30% cash cap), the residual arb spread is negligible-to-negative and there is no currently-unpriced figure (fails G3) — downgrade to WATCH/low score until/unless a wider spread re-opens.

verified.catalyst: NGN-401 gene therapy for Rett syndrome: registrational Embolden topline data is the binary, but the near-term catalysts (dosing completion, BTD, interim Phase 1/2 data) have already fired.
verified.dated_milestone: Embolden registrational topline data expected 2H 2027 (after first 24 participants reach 12-mo follow-up; success bar = 8/24 composite responders); BLA submission planned after that. Interim Phase 1/2 data (cutoff 6/16/2026) presents at IRSF 6/29–7/1/2026.
verified.bloom_gates: G2 (concrete registrational-trial commitment with a defined success threshold) — but the dated near-term legs have fired; only the distant 2H-2027 readout remains forward.
verified.edge: L — the triage thesis is stale/wrong (dosing already completed 6/8/2026, BTD already granted, interim data firing at IRSF this week), and the stock has re-rated to $32.28 (near 52wk high $37, +38% vs 200d avg $23.4) on the positive Phase 1/2 run, pricing in the good interim signal ahead of the real binary.
verified.primary_source: Neurogene press release "Successful Completion of Dosing in Embolden Registrational Trial of NGN-401," BusinessWire/BioSpace, June 8, 2026; interim data cutoff June 16, 2026 (IRSF 6/29–7/1/2026); FMP quote $32.28 (6/29/2026).
verified.analysis: The triage note is factually stale: it claims "dosing completes Q2 2026 + data mid-2026 → BLA," but Embolden dosing already completed (June 8, 2026), BTD was already granted, and the mid-2026 interim Phase 1/2 data (cutoff June 16, 2026) is being presented at IRSF June 29–July 1, 2026 — i.e., firing today, not forward. The only genuinely forward dated binary is the registrational topline expected 2H 2027 (with BLA after), which is ~15+ months out, while the stock has already re-rated near its 52-week high on the strong long-term Phase 1/2 milestone data. Real and dated, but the asymmetry has largely been consumed by the re-rate and the near catalysts have fired, so this is a WATCH on the distant 2H-2027 readout rather than a fresh ACTIVE setup.

verified.catalyst: Ingevity is running a sale process for its Advanced Polymer Technologies (APT) segment under a "New Ingevity" portfolio-simplification push, but with no named buyer, no signed deal, and no hard dated milestone.
verified.dated_milestone: No hard date. Soft management aspiration to "announce something before end of 2026" (stated Q4 call, Feb 28 2026); next checkpoint is Q2 2026 earnings (~late July/early Aug 2026), but no committed APT-deal date.
verified.bloom_gates: None met. G1 (named counterparty): fail — no buyer disclosed for APT. G2 (concrete dated commitment): fail — only a soft, undated "by end of 2026" aspiration. G3 (specific currently-unpriced figure): fail — no APT SoP/valuation disclosed.
verified.edge: L — the portfolio-simplification thesis is well-publicized and the one concrete deal (Ozark to PPG, $65M) has already closed and fired; stock at $74 near 52-wk high ($79.29) vs low $39.74 and 200-day $64, so the monetization narrative is substantially re-rated/priced in.
verified.primary_source: Ingevity portfolio-review press release (Dec 8, 2025, businesswire.com); Ozark/PPG sale close (Apr 15, 2026, businesswire.com); Q1 2026 call portfolio-simplification commentary (May 12-13, 2026); FMP quote $74 (Jun 29, 2026)
verified.analysis: NGVT's restructuring is real and ongoing — Ozark Materials road-markings sold to PPG for $65M (closed Apr 15, 2026) — but that piece has already fired and re-rated the stock to near 52-week highs. The remaining live item, an APT-segment sale, has no named counterparty, no signed agreement, and only a soft "announce before end of 2026" management aspiration rather than any hard dated milestone. Fails all three Bloom gates under DEEP strictness; this is the "ongoing monetization, no single dated hard milestone" pattern the triage flagged, so NONE.

verified.catalyst: Bécancour 13-ktpa battery-material plant final investment decision targeted for H2-2026, but still only in "financing modeling / active engagement with targeted financial partners" — no named counterparty or committed term sheet.
verified.dated_milestone: Bécancour Battery Material Plant FID, targeted H2-2026 (no firm date; soft "second half 2026" guidance)
verified.bloom_gates: none cleanly met — G1 no named Bécancour-financing counterparty yet; G2 no concrete commitment (only "active engagement"); G3 no specific currently-unpriced figure (Matawinie's $335M/$213M already disclosed and priced)
verified.edge: L — the dated, counterparty-backed catalyst (Matawinie $335M senior debt + $213M CGF/IQ/Eni equity + Canada/Eni offtakes) already CLOSED and FID was confirmed May 15, 2026, with construction groundbreaking May 19; the market sold the news (down 24% in 4 wks, $1.51 vs $6.06 52wk-high, below 50/200d MAs), so the asymmetry is gone. Remaining Bécancour FID is a soft H2-2026 funding-risk milestone, not unpriced special-sit asymmetry.
verified.primary_source: Business Wire via FMP — "NMG Completes Previously Announced US$309.5M Equity Financing Package Confirming FID for the Phase-2 Matawinie Mine" (2026-05-15) and "Officially Launches Construction... Ground-Breaking Ceremony" (2026-05-19); Bécancour H2-2026 FID target per April 2026 6-K financing release.
verified.analysis: The triage-flagged catalyst is already fired: NMG closed its US$309.5M equity package (incl. US$213M from Canada Growth Fund/Investissement Québec/Eni) on May 15, 2026, confirmed FID on Phase-2 Matawinie, signed a definitive Government-of-Canada offtake May 13, and held a construction groundbreaking May 19 with PM Carney — all known, dilutive (3x 44.45M new shares) and re-rated, with the stock down to $1.51 (52wk low $1.37). The only forward item is the 13-ktpa Bécancour plant FID "targeted H2-2026," but it remains in financing modeling with no named partner, no committed facility and no firm date, which is financing/FID risk rather than dated unpriced asymmetry. Fails the strict gate (fired + re-rated main catalyst; soft, counterparty-less forward milestone) so NONE / score 3.

verified.catalyst: FCC rulemaking on reconfiguring the 902-928 MHz Lower 900 MHz band to grant NextNav a 15 MHz nationwide PNT license — a contested spectrum swap whose grant would unlock a multi-billion-dollar band value not fully priced.
verified.dated_milestone: Draft NPRM at OMB/OIRA since March 2026 awaiting clearance; next gate = FCC open-meeting adoption of the NPRM (not yet calendared — July 2026 agenda posts ~mid-July; triage's ~Apr-30 date appears to have slipped).
verified.bloom_gates: G3 (specific unpriced figure: 15 MHz nationwide license in 902-928 MHz, contested by a 60-org coalition); G1 partial (named counterparty = FCC/OMB), but no concrete dated commitment.
verified.edge: M — genuine asymmetry on a grant, but the near-term event (NPRM adoption) is only a proposal-to-consider, the resolving binary (license grant) is undated and years out, and spectrum optionality is already partly in the ~$2.4B cap; heavy organized opposition raises denial/dilution risk.
verified.primary_source: FCC draft NPRM to OMB/OIRA reported March 2026 (stocksfoundry/powermag); RILA 60-org coalition opposition press release June 2026 (rila.org); ITIF House Subcommittee testimony June 4, 2026 (itif.org).
verified.analysis: The catalyst is real, specific and forward: the FCC sent a draft NPRM to OMB in March 2026 to open rulemaking on reconfiguring the Lower 900 MHz band for NextNav's 15 MHz nationwide PNT license, and a June 4, 2026 House subcommittee hearing plus a 60-org opposition coalition (RILA et al.) confirm it is live and contested. But it fails the "hard dated binary" bar: the NPRM has sat at OMB ~3 months with no adopted-NPRM date (the triage ~Apr-30 meeting date did not materialize), and even adoption merely launches a comment period rather than resolving the binary — the actual license grant is undated and likely years away. Net: a legitimate regulatory optionality story but a slow grind with a slipping/soft next date and partial pricing, so WATCH rather than ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: Cash-rich shell (~$441.6M liquidity vs ~$301M market cap) running a Guggenheim/Houlihan strategic review under Murchinson activist pressure, with a Phase 3 "path forward" decision pending.
verified.dated_milestone: Phase 3 strategic-path decision: CEO (June 5, 2026 letter) guided "in the coming weeks" / a series of Q2-2026 actions — no fixed date; Markforged/Stratasys $42.5M close 2H 2026.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparties: Murchinson 13D/A, Guggenheim, Houlihan Lokey, Stratasys) + G3 (trades below net cash: ~$441.6M liquidity vs ~$301M mkt cap, as of Mar 31, 2026). G2 monetization legs (Markforged $42.5M, AME/Fabrica $12.5M) already FIRED, not forward.
verified.edge: M — sub-net-cash valuation with a live activist + banker-run process is a genuine unpriced special-sit, but capital-return path is unconfirmed and dilution/history is messy.
verified.primary_source: Nano Dimension CEO letter, GlobeNewswire/SEC 8-K, June 5, 2026 (Phase 3 "coming weeks"; $441.6M liquidity); shareholder letter May 26, 2026 (Murchinson 13D/A); Markforged-Stratasys $42.5M agreement (2H-2026 close)
verified.analysis: NNDM is a legitimate cash-rich shell (~$441.6M liquidity at Mar 31, 2026 vs ~$301M market cap at $1.43, near 52-wk low) with a banker-run strategic review (Guggenheim + Houlihan Lokey) under an active Murchinson 13D/A control campaign — a real, named activist/restructuring setup trading below net cash (G1+G3). The forward catalyst is the Phase 3 "path forward" decision the CEO guided for "the coming weeks" / Q2-2026 in his June 5, 2026 letter, but the milestone is softly dated (no hard date), the monetization legs (Markforged $42.5M to Stratasys, AME/Fabrica $12.5M to Inspira) are already announced/fired, and there is no concrete capital-return commitment yet. Real and forward but loosely dated and messy — WATCH pending a hard-dated path-forward announcement.

verified.catalyst: Definitive $750M de-SPAC merging New Providence Acquisition Corp III into Abra Financial (to list as ABRX on Nasdaq), pending S-4 effectiveness and a shareholder vote.
verified.dated_milestone: Definitive BCA signed 2026-03-16; next gate = S-4 effectiveness + EGM shareholder vote — no specific date set as of late June 2026 (S-4 not yet declared effective; no EGM scheduled).
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Abra Financial Holdings) and G2 (concrete definitive BCA, $750M pre-money, $40M min net-trust + $150M financing target) PASS; G3 FAILS — no specific currently-unpriced figure accrues to the NPAC holder, who sits at trust value.
verified.edge: L — at $10.37 the stock is pinned at trust/redemption value, so the SPAC-arb holder has a capped, near-zero forward payoff; post-close equity is a speculative crypto-RIA bet (Abra's prior SEC settlements + retail shutdown) exposed to heavy redemption and de-SPAC value destruction, not a defined unpriced upside.
verified.primary_source: SEC Form 425 / NPAC 8-K, business combination announcement (filed 2026-03-17, agreement dated 2026-03-16); FMP quote NPAC $10.37 (timestamp 2026-06-29).
verified.analysis: The catalyst is real, named, and forward: a definitive March 16, 2026 business combination agreement to merge NPAC into Abra Financial at a $750M pre-money valuation, listing as ABRX, with up to ~$300M trust cash, a $40M min net-trust condition and a $150M financing target. But it is not yet dated to a specific vote — the S-4 is not effective and no extraordinary general meeting has been scheduled as of late June 2026 — and the NPAC common trades at $10.37, i.e. trust value, so there is no unpriced upside for the holder (G3 fails). The instrument is SPAC arb with a capped payoff and the post-close thesis is a speculative, heavily-redemption-and-regulatory-risked crypto-RIA equity, so this gates to a low-conviction WATCH rather than a fireable catalyst.

verified.catalyst: Bexobrutideg (BTK degrader) registrational path: pivotal Phase 2 DAYBreak CLL-201 designed to support Accelerated Approval in triple-exposed r/r CLL, with the confirmatory Phase 3 DAYBreak CLL-306 to start by midyear 2026.
verified.dated_milestone: Phase 3 DAYBreak CLL-306 initiation guided "by midyear 2026" (next dated step, ~Q2/Q3 2026); the value-inflection Phase 2 AA-data readout is itself UNDATED (no topline-data or NDA-filing date disclosed).
verified.bloom_gates: G2 (concrete commitment: company-guided Phase 3 confirmatory start by midyear 2026 and an AA-designed pivotal Phase 2 already enrolling). G3 partial only — the unpriced figure (AA submission / topline ORR) lacks a disclosed date. No G1 (no named counterparty / partner / acquirer).
verified.edge: L — much of the BTK-degrader thesis is already priced: stock $23.85 sits at the 52-wk high ($24.03), up ~3x from the $8.20 low and well above the $15-17 50/200-day averages, with $2.07B cap. The only firmly DATED milestone is a trial start (low surprise value), while the real binary (Phase 2 AA data) has no public readout date — so limited unpriced, time-bounded edge.
verified.primary_source: Nurix Q1 2026 results / corporate update, GlobeNewswire 2026-04-08 (Phase 3 CLL-306 "by midyear 2026", $540.7M cash as of Feb 28 2026); corroborated by Nurix 2026 Goals release 2026-01-12 and DAYBreak Phase 2 initiation PR 2025-10-22; FMP quote 2026-06-29 ($23.85, 52-wk high $24.03).
verified.analysis: Nurix's bexobrutideg has a genuine registrational binary — an Accelerated-Approval-designed pivotal Phase 2 (DAYBreak CLL-201, enrolling since Oct 2025) plus a confirmatory Phase 3 (DAYBreak CLL-306) the company has repeatedly guided to start "by midyear 2026" (Jan 12 and April 8 2026 releases). But it is a clinical/regulatory development bet, not a special situation: there is no named counterparty (G1) and, critically, no disclosed date for the value-inflection Phase 2 topline/NDA, so the catalyst is forward but only loosely time-bounded. With the stock at its 52-wk high after a ~3x Phase-1-driven re-rating and a well-funded $540.7M balance sheet, the near-term dated step (a Phase 3 start) carries little unpriced upside, supporting WATCH rather than ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: Public Storage all-stock acquisition of National Storage Affiliates (0.14 PSA/NSA share), pending NSA holder vote and expected Q3 2026 close.
verified.dated_milestone: NSA shareholder special-meeting vote (record date June 1, 2026); close expected Q3 2026.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Public Storage) + G2 (boards-approved definitive merger agreement, S-4/424B3 filed)
verified.edge: L — spread is essentially closed: NSA $45.35 vs deal value 0.14*PSA $324.45 = $45.42, ~0.16% gross, no PSA-borrow/dividend cushion left; the "$41.68" in triage is the stale Mar-13 implied price, PSA has re-rated up.
verified.primary_source: SEC Form 424B3 / S-4 (PSA, EDGAR, filed 2026, record date June 1, 2026); Business Wire deal announcement Mar 16, 2026; FMP live quotes NSA $45.35 / PSA $324.45 (2026-06-29).
verified.analysis: The deal is real, dated and forward (board-approved Mar 16, 2026; S-4/424B3 effective, June 1 record date, holder vote pending, Q3 2026 close) and clears Bloom G1/G2 — but it fails the unpriced test. With PSA at $324.45 the fixed-ratio consideration is worth ~$45.42 versus NSA at $45.35, a ~0.16% gross spread that does not cover PSA short-borrow/dividend carry for a hedged arb. The catalyst is effectively fired/re-rated into the tape; the $41.68 cited is the announcement-day value, not the current parity, so there is no remaining edge.

verified.catalyst: Union Pacific's $85B acquisition of Norfolk Southern (1.0 UNP share + $88.82 cash per NSC share) is live at the STB, which accepted the amended application May 28, 2026 and set a binary regulatory decision path toward an early-2027 close.
verified.dated_milestone: Applicants' supplemental information due to STB July 27, 2026 (proceedings/environmental review in abeyance until then); next hard gate, then STB ruling toward early-2027 close.
verified.instrument: NSC common (merger-arb long vs. UNP short, given the 1.0x stock component)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Union Pacific) + G2 (concrete binding commitment: signed deal, 1.0 UNP + $88.82 cash, $2.5B reverse termination fee, NSC shareholder approval secured) + G3 (specific unpriced figure: ~$44.87/sh gross spread)
verified.primary_source: STB PR-26-13 / Railway Age, "STB Accepts UP-NS Revised Merger Application; Delays Proceedings," 2026-05-28/29; deal terms per UP press release 2025-07-29 and S-4/424B3.
verified.kill_fact: STB primary source (pr-26-13, dated May 28, 2026) confirms the Board accepted UP's revised application to control NSC AND simultaneously held the entire proceeding — including environmental review — in abeyance, ordering supplemental information by July 27, 2026. Terms (1.0 UNP share + $88.82 cash, ~$320/sh, $85B EV, early-2027 expected close) confirmed via UP/NSC releases and SEC 424B3; NSC shareholders already approved, so remaining gating risk is purely the STB binary. Catalyst is real, dated, and forward (deal pending, not closed). CORRECTION/nuance: acceptance was conditional and the statutory review clock is effectively paused (abeyance) until the 7/27 supplemental filing, so 'early-2027 close' is softer than a clean accepted-application implies — but the line already prices this as hard-to-handicap STB risk, so it stands.
verified.analysis: UP is acquiring NSC for 1.0 UNP share + $88.82 cash; at UNP $272.70 the deal implies ~$361.5/sh vs NSC's $316.65, a ~$44.9 (~14%) gross spread to an expected early-2027 close. The catalyst is real, dated and forward — STB accepted the amended application 2026-05-28, held the case in abeyance, and requires supplemental data by 2026-07-27 before the substantive review and ruling. This is a legitimate regulatory-binary merger-arb (not a macro bet): edge is medium because the wide spread compensates for true, hard-to-handicap STB-approval risk on an unprecedented transcontinental Class I combination, and the stock-heavy consideration requires a UNP hedge to isolate.

verified.catalyst: Butterfield signed a definitive agreement May 27-28 2026 to acquire CIBC's 91.7% stake in CIBC Caribbean for ~$1.794B (61% cash / 39% stock), guided to 12% GAAP / 15% cash EPS accretion and 10% TBVPS accretion at close.
verified.dated_milestone: Butterfield shareholder vote (share-issuance approval) + multi-jurisdiction regulatory approvals; next date undisclosed, expected close H1 2027.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty CIBC) + G2 (signed definitive agreement, quantified accretion) PASS; forward/unpriced test FAILS — NTB is acquirer, no arb spread, deal-pop already realized.
verified.edge: L — confirmed and accurate but NTB is the acquirer (no takeout/arb spread); accretion is management guidance already partly capitalized (stock +5.4% 1M, +16% 3M, +19.5% YTD post-May-28), so the catalyst has substantially fired and the residual is a slow ~12-mo integration re-rating, not a discrete unpriced binary.
verified.primary_source: Butterfield 6-K press release / Businesswire, "Butterfield Announces Agreement to Acquire Control of CIBC Caribbean in $1.8 Billion Transaction," May 28, 2026 (deal value, 12% GAAP/15% cash EPS accretion, 10% TBVPS accretion, H1 2027 close); FMP quote-change as of 2026-06-29.
verified.analysis: The $1.8B CIBC Caribbean acquisition is real, specific and dated (definitive agreement 5/27-28/26, close H1 2027 pending shareholder + regulatory votes), satisfying Bloom G1/G2, but the triage caveat is correct: NTB is the buyer, so there is no merger-arb spread to harvest and the upside is a structural re-rating on accretive scale (12% GAAP EPS, 10% TBVPS accretion). The deal-announcement re-rating has already largely fired — NTB is up ~5.4% 1M / ~16% 3M / ~19.5% YTD with the stock now near multi-year highs — leaving the remaining leg a slow integration story rather than a clean forward unpriced figure (no specific G3 spread/figure that is currently unpriced). Holds as WATCH only: monitor for a dated shareholder-meeting/regulatory-approval calendar that could create a discrete re-rate or financing-overhang event, but it is not an ACTIVE catalyst.

verified.catalyst: Two dated, forward FDA approval binaries for Nuvalent (NUVL): zidesamtinib (ROS1+ NSCLC) PDUFA Sept 18, 2026 and neladalkib (ALK+ NSCLC) PDUFA Nov 27, 2026, each with a commercial launch immediately on deck.
verified.dated_milestone: Zidesamtinib PDUFA target action date Sept 18, 2026 (next); followed by neladalkib PDUFA Nov 27, 2026
verified.instrument: equity (NUVL, NASDAQ); long-dated calls viable given binary structure
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty = FDA, specific drug/indication) + G2 (concrete commitment = NDA accepted, PDUFA dates set, breakthrough/priority designations, launch planned 2026)
verified.edge: M — both events are dated and well-telegraphed (already partly in a ~$9.1B mkt cap near 52-wk high $123.69 vs low $71.13), so approval base case is increasingly consensus; edge is in launch ramp/label breadth and the back-to-back catalyst cadence rather than a mispriced binary; not a clean unpriced surprise.
verified.primary_source: Nuvalent OnTarget 2026 milestones PR / FDA NDA acceptance (zidesamtinib PDUFA Sept 18, 2026), Jan 12, 2026; Nuvalent neladalkib NDA acceptance + Priority Review PDUFA Nov 27, 2026, May 27, 2026 (investors.nuvalent.com)
verified.kill_fact: Both PDUFAs verified vs primary sources: zidesamtinib (ROS1+ NSCLC) NDA accepted, PDUFA Sept 18, 2026 (Nuvalent IR/PRNewswire Nov 19, 2025); neladalkib (ALK+ NSCLC) NDA accepted + Priority Review, PDUFA Nov 27, 2026 (Nuvalent IR May 27, 2026). Ticker confirmed NUVL=Nuvalent (NOT Nuvation Bio); triage misID already corrected in the line. Both are dated, forward, unfired binaries with 2026 launch on deck. Caveat holds: NUVL $123.51 sits AT the 52-week high ($123.69), far above 50d ($107.57)/200d ($100.77) MAs — approval probability is substantially priced, capping edge to medium.
verified.analysis: Triage misidentified the issuer as "Nuvation Bio" — this is Nuvalent (NUVL). The flag is also partly stale: the neladalkib ALK NDA was not just "filed H2 2026" but was submitted Apr 7, accepted with Priority Review May 27, and now carries a dated Nov 27, 2026 PDUFA — so it is a fully dated forward binary, not a pending filing. Combined with the zidesamtinib Sept 18, 2026 PDUFA and 2026 commercial launch, NUVL has two genuine near-term, dated, unfired approval catalysts; gate passes on G1+G2, though much of the approval probability appears priced (stock near 52-week high), capping edge to medium.

verified.catalyst: Pivotal Phase 3 OPERA-01 topline of palazestrant monotherapy in 2L/3L ER+/HER2- metastatic breast cancer (post-CDK4/6), a true unfired clinical binary.
verified.dated_milestone: OPERA-01 monotherapy PFS topline expected fall 2026 (next date: fall 2026; confirmed at Q1 2026 earnings, May 2026)
verified.instrument: equity (NASDAQ: OLMA); long-dated calls for the binary if liquid
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (Novartis named counterparty on OPERA-02 combo/supply agreement) + G3 (unpriced Phase 3 approval/launch optionality — stock ~$12 vs $36 52w-high implies the readout is not priced in)
verified.edge: M — real, specific, dated, forward binary with funded balance sheet (no financing overhang into the event) and depressed tape, but a Phase 3 oncology PFS readout in a tough post-CDK4/6 setting is a high-variance ~coin-flip, not a high-probability edge.
verified.primary_source: Olema Oncology Q1 2026 earnings release / StockTitan, May 2026 — "Top-line data from the pivotal Phase 3 OPERA-01 trial of palazestrant as a monotherapy expected in fall 2026"
verified.analysis: OLMA is Olema Oncology; the triage flag is confirmed accurate — pivotal Phase 3 OPERA-01 topline for palazestrant monotherapy in 2L/3L ER+/HER2- mBC is expected fall 2026, reiterated at the Q1 2026 earnings release. It is genuinely forward and unfired (no topline reported as of 2026-06-29), idiosyncratic (not macro), and asymmetric: ~$1.07B cap, $505M cash vs ~$53M/qtr burn (funded through the event, no dilution forced before the readout), and stock at $12.20 vs a $36 52-week high signals the market is not pricing success. Caveat: cap is just over the "sub-$1B" framing, and a monotherapy PFS bar after prior CDK4/6 is the hardest setting, so this is a high-variance binary warranting WATCH (not ACTIVE) until closer to the readout window.
